title: Build and Ship Accessible Asteroids with Python 3.14 and pygame-ce 2.5.7
description: Python and object-oriented programming learners who need to design, build, test, package, playtest, and defend a complete accessible real-time 2D game rather than copy a monolithic arcade tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7; sdl boundaries; initialization and game loops; vector2 and explicit units; variable and fixed time steps; events, held input, remapping, focus and controllers; scenes; ship physics; toroidal wrapping; responsive viewports; surfaces, draw order, transforms, and assets; projectiles; seeded asteroid geometry; layered collision detection and response; scoring, waves, safe spawns, and difficulty; sprites and groups; audio; accessible hud and menus; deterministic replay and saves; pure-core architecture; unit, property, headless, render, and playtest evidence; performance; pyinstaller 6.21 packaging; local security and privacy; release, rollback, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit native pygame-ce, sdl, display, input, audio, asset, file, platform, profiler, package, accessibility playtest, performance, recovery, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic game deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-platform defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves player goal, revision, Python and pygame-ce versions, explicit units, seed, input and time trace, state invariant, bounded entities and work, accessible multichannel feedback, changed-case tests, failure and recovery evidence, and explicit browser-versus-desktop limits before adding one game boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, asset, filesystem, GPU, frame-pacing, installer, or release behavior; those require controlled desktop evidence.

Passing work requires a stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate state evidence, at least one changed frame-rate, seam, input, collision, device, accessibility, or failure case, exact observable results, a test that detects a deliberate defect, and a named owner for remaining risk.

competencies.statement: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
misconceptions: A recognizable triangle and rocks constitute a complete game outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.

competencies.statement: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
misconceptions: Fun cannot be investigated with observable evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.

competencies.statement: Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
misconceptions: A game prototype is too small to need a clean repository baseline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.

competencies.statement: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.
misconceptions: Accessibility is a post-release options-menu polish task.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.

competencies.statement: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
misconceptions: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

competencies.statement: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
misconceptions: Classic pygame and pygame-ce can safely coexist in one environment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.

competencies.statement: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
misconceptions: Pygame is a game engine that owns every application decision.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.

competencies.statement: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.
misconceptions: pygame.init returning is proof that every subsystem works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.

competencies.statement: Order event intake, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, presentation, and timing once per frame.
misconceptions: Any loop order is equivalent if the screen changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order event intake, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, presentation, and timing once per frame.

competencies.statement: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.
misconceptions: Closing the terminal is an adequate game shutdown design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

competencies.statement: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
misconceptions: Positive y always means visually upward.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.

competencies.statement: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
misconceptions: Every Vector2 operation returns a new value and cannot alias state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.

competencies.statement: Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
misconceptions: Vector2.rotate visually follows the usual mathematical counterclockwise direction on screen.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.

competencies.statement: Guard normalization and scale-to-length operations when a vector has no direction.

competencies.statement: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

competencies.statement: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
misconceptions: Clock.tick returns seconds and enforces exact frame pacing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.

competencies.statement: Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.

competencies.statement: Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.
misconceptions: A fixed step means sleeping for a fixed amount before every update.

competencies.statement: Clamp pathological frame time and cap catch-up updates while recording discarded debt and player impact.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Clamp pathological frame time and cap catch-up updates while recording discarded debt and player impact.

competencies.statement: Replay identical inputs across 30, 60, 144, jittered, paused, and stalled presentation traces and compare state tolerances.

competencies.statement: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

competencies.statement: Integrate acceleration into velocity and velocity into position with explicit seconds and bounded time.

competencies.statement: Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.

competencies.statement: Clamp vector magnitude rather than components and define reverse, zero, and boost behavior.

competencies.statement: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

competencies.statement: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.

competencies.statement: Compare shortest toroidal displacement or ghost proxies so collisions remain continuous across seams.

competencies.statement: Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.

competencies.statement: Load licensed assets through versioned paths, validate dimensions and formats, provide fallbacks, and surface causal failures.

competencies.statement: Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.
misconceptions: A neon palette is readable and accessible by default.

competencies.statement: Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.

competencies.statement: Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.

competencies.statement: Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.

competencies.statement: Expire projectiles by world distance or simulation time and remove every group and collision reference exactly once.

competencies.statement: If reuse is justified, reset all position, velocity, age, owner, collision, render, and active fields and prove reconciliation.
misconceptions: Object pooling is automatically faster and safe if instances are recycled.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: If reuse is justified, reset all position, velocity, age, owner, collision, render, and active fields and prove reconciliation.

competencies.statement: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
misconceptions: Calling random.seed once makes an entire program reproducible regardless of code changes.

competencies.statement: Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
misconceptions: Random x-y points connected in generation order always form an asteroid outline.

competencies.statement: Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.

competencies.statement: Sample spawn edges or zones under distance, visibility, seam, entity-cap, retry, and safe-fallback constraints.

competencies.statement: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.
misconceptions: Difficulty is one multiplier applied to every number.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.

competencies.statement: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.
misconceptions: Pixel-perfect collision is always the fairest and best choice.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.

competencies.statement: Use groups, spatial partitioning, sweep, or bounded pair sets to reduce candidates without dropping possible overlaps.
misconceptions: With a small prototype, quadratic collision work never needs a budget.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use groups, spatial partitioning, sweep, or bounded pair sets to reduce candidates without dropping possible overlaps.

competencies.statement: Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
misconceptions: Distance must always take a square root to be correct.

competencies.statement: Build masks only from current transformed surfaces when needed and align offsets to matching coordinate systems.
misconceptions: spritecollide with a mask automatically understands world centers and rotation.

competencies.statement: Test segment or swept motion and toroidal images for fast movement and edge crossings.

competencies.statement: Separate collision facts from response decisions so detection stays pure and effects occur in an owned phase.
misconceptions: Collision detection should immediately mutate every involved object.

competencies.statement: Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.

competencies.statement: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.
misconceptions: Pure functions cannot express a responsive real-time game.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.

competencies.statement: Keep display, events, devices, audio, assets, persistence, clock, logging, and packaging behind thin owned adapters.
misconceptions: Wrapping pygame calls in classes automatically creates clean architecture.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep display, events, devices, audio, assets, persistence, clock, logging, and packaging behind thin owned adapters.

competencies.statement: Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.
misconceptions: An event bus removes the need to define event ownership and ordering.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.

competencies.statement: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.
misconceptions: Small games benefit from global state because architecture is unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.

competencies.statement: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality.
misconceptions: Headless import success proves the complete desktop game works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove the pure core runs without pygame and the adapter composition runs headlessly without claiming visual or device quality.

competencies.statement: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.
misconceptions: Testing individual methods establishes end-to-end game correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.

competencies.statement: Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.
misconceptions: Random tests are useful even without reproducible seeds or invariants.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.

competencies.statement: Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
misconceptions: SDL dummy drivers accurately simulate player hardware.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.

competencies.statement: Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.
misconceptions: One pixel-perfect screenshot is a stable cross-platform rendering test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.

competencies.statement: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
misconceptions: Automated accessibility checks can certify game playability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.

competencies.statement: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
misconceptions: Average FPS reveals every visible hitch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.

competencies.statement: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
misconceptions: The profiler identifies which change to make without a performance question.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.

competencies.statement: Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.
misconceptions: Object allocation is always the main Python game bottleneck.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.

competencies.statement: Measure candidate and narrow-phase pairs, entity caps, spatial index cost, and changed distribution cases.
misconceptions: The same collision algorithm scales linearly until the screen looks crowded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure candidate and narrow-phase pairs, entity caps, spatial index cost, and changed distribution cases.

competencies.statement: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
misconceptions: A faster build is acceptable if it merely feels the same.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.

competencies.statement: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
misconceptions: A requirements file and main.py fully describe a distributable game.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.

competencies.statement: Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
misconceptions: Relative asset paths behave the same from source and a bundled executable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.

competencies.statement: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
misconceptions: PyInstaller cross-compiles Windows and macOS executables from Linux.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.

competencies.statement: Test supported OS, architecture, scaling, display, audio, input, permissions, offline, locale, and clean-profile combinations.

competencies.statement: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.
misconceptions: Free-to-download game assets are automatically distributable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.

competencies.statement: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
misconceptions: An offline single-player game has no security boundary.

competencies.statement: Constrain asset, settings, replay, and score paths to approved roots and reject traversal, symlink, type, and size violations.
misconceptions: Joining a trusted directory with a filename prevents path escape.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain asset, settings, replay, and score paths to approved roots and reject traversal, symlink, type, and size violations.

competencies.statement: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.
misconceptions: Local pickle data is safe because only the player can edit it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.

competencies.statement: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.
misconceptions: A small compressed file cannot exhaust local resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.

competencies.statement: Minimize diagnostics, redact paths and identifiers, require informed opt-in for telemetry, and document retention and deletion.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Minimize diagnostics, redact paths and identifiers, require informed opt-in for telemetry, and document retention and deletion.

competencies.statement: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
misconceptions: Collecting positive playtest comments is sufficient launch evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

competencies.statement: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.

competencies.statement: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
misconceptions: Restarting the game is an adequate recovery plan for every failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.

competencies.statement: Retain verified prior artifacts and compatible save handling, rehearse downgrade boundaries, and define abort and restore decisions.
misconceptions: Rebuilding an old Git tag produces a guaranteed identical rollback artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain verified prior artifacts and compatible save handling, rehearse downgrade boundaries, and define abort and restore decisions.

competencies.statement: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.
misconceptions: A final high-score run proves production readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.
